text.title: PMBoK 7 and 8 Reference Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the ref-pmbok methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Always show formula AND result when reporting EVM metrics. Never emit only the computed value.
rule.rationale: Audit trails require reproducibility; a bare '$92K EAC' without formula cannot be verified or challenged.

rule.statement: Clamp EVM denominators to avoid division by zero. When PV or AC is zero, emit null for affected metrics rather than infinity or NaN.
rule.rationale: LLM arithmetic on near-zero denominators produces nonsense results that look plausible.

rule.statement: Present estimation accuracy bands as heuristics with ranges, never as guarantees; name the technique alongside the band.
rule.rationale: Sponsors anchor on the tightest band regardless of technique used; the band is technique-dependent.

rule.statement: Pair every risk entry with exactly one response strategy. A risk without a strategy is an observation, not a managed risk.
rule.rationale: Risk registers without strategies are decoration.

rule.statement: If more than 50% of projects in a portfolio are amber, the RAG threshold is miscalibrated, not the projects. Recalibrate before acting.
rule.rationale: RAG inflation destroys the signal; amber must be rare enough to trigger genuine attention.

rule.statement: Pin the PMBoK edition in every project charter and in every agent system prompt that generates PM content.
rule.rationale: PMBoK 6 is over-represented in training data; without explicit pinning, agents default to 6th edition vocabulary.

list.item: As a standalone methodology to do anything — pair with operational methodologies
Non-PMI frameworks (PRINCE2, IPMA, ISO 21500) — vocabulary overlaps but differs
Agile-only environments with no baseline — EVM formulae are meaningless
